articles.title: Tracking recent US-Israeli strikes on Iranian infrastructure
articles.description: Attacks have targeted bridges, steel plants and pharmaceutical facilities, verified videos show.
articles.titleJa: 米国とイスラエルによるイランのインフラへの最近の攻撃を追跡
articles.summary: 米国とイスラエルがイランの橋、製鉄所、製薬施設を標的に攻撃したことを報じ、検証済みの動画で確認された。これは軍事衝突の激化を示す。

articles.title: Wireless Festival cancelled after Kanye West blocked from coming to UK
articles.description: West was due to headline the festival in July but drew criticism over past antisemitic comments.
articles.titleJa: カニエ・ウェストの英国入国拒否を受け、ワイヤレス・フェスティバルが中止に
articles.summary: カニエ・ウェストの過去の反ユダヤ主義的な発言が批判を浴び、英国入国が阻止されたため、彼がヘッドライナーを務める予定だったワイヤレス・フェスティバルが中止された。

articles.title: Has Artemis II shown we can land on the Moon again?
articles.description: The Artemis II mission has been near flawless to date, but has the test flight shown Nasa is ready to send humans to the lunar surface?
articles.titleJa: アルテミスIIは月面への再着陸が可能であることを示したのか？
articles.summary: アルテミスIIミッションはほぼ完璧に進んでいるが、この試験飛行がNASAが再び人類を月面へ送る準備が整っていることを示したのかが問われている。

articles.title: US journalist Shelly Kittleson to be released after kidnap in Iraq, militia says
articles.description: Kataib Hezbollah says the release comes on condition that she leave Iraq immediately.
articles.titleJa: 米国人ジャーナリスト、シェリー・キトルソンがイラクでの誘拐後解放へ、民兵組織が発表
articles.summary: イラクで誘拐された米国人ジャーナリスト、シェリー・キトルソンが、民兵組織の発表により解放されることになった。解放の条件は彼女が直ちにイラクを離れること。

articles.title: One gunman killed and two injured in shooting at Israeli consulate in Istanbul
articles.description: No Israeli diplomats are currently in Turkey and the Istanbul consulate has been empty for the past two-and-a-half years.

articles.title: JD Vance backs Orbán's re-election bid in Budapest visit and hits out at EU
articles.description: US Vice-President JD Vance intervenes in the campaign to give Orbán a ringing endorsement in Budapest.
articles.titleJa: JDヴァンス、ブダペスト訪問でオルバン氏の再選を支持し、EUを非難
articles.summary: 米副大統領JDヴァンスがブダペストを訪問し、オルバン氏の再選を強く支持するとともにEUを非難し、選挙戦に介入した。

articles.title: WHO suspends Gaza medical evacuations after contractor killed by Israeli troops
articles.description: Israel's military says troops fired at a vehicle driven by the Palestinian because they believed it posed "an immediate threat".
articles.titleJa: WHO、イスラエル軍による請負業者殺害を受けガザの医療避難を一時停止
articles.summary: イスラエル軍による請負業者の殺害を受け、世界保健機関（WHO）はガザ地区からの医療避難を一時停止した。イスラエル軍は車両が「差し迫った脅威」と判断し発砲したと述べた。

articles.title: Chilean woman accused of Pinochet-era kidnaps loses Australia extradition battle
articles.description: Adriana Rivas moved from Chile to Australia in the 1970s, working there as a nanny and cleaner.
articles.titleJa: ピノチェト時代の誘拐で告発されたチリ人女性、豪州での引き渡し巡る裁判に敗訴
articles.summary: ピノチェト時代に誘拐に関与したとして告発されたチリ人女性が、オーストラリアでの引き渡しを巡る法廷闘争に敗訴した。彼女は1970年代にチリからオーストラリアに移住していた。

articles.title: One dead as train travelling 99mph collides with lorry in France
articles.description: The lorry driver is in custody after the train driver died during the collision in northern France.
articles.titleJa: フランスで時速159キロの列車がトラックと衝突、1人死亡
articles.summary: フランス北部で、時速約159キロで走行中の列車がトラックと衝突し、列車運転士が死亡した。トラック運転手は拘留されている。

articles.title: Australia's most-decorated living soldier charged over alleged war crimes
articles.description: Ben Roberts-Smith, who denies all wrongdoing, previously lost a landmark defamation case over the alleged murders.
articles.titleJa: オーストラリアで最も勲章を持つ現役兵士が戦争犯罪の疑いで起訴
articles.summary: オーストラリアで最も勲章を受勲している現役兵士が戦争犯罪の疑いで起訴された。彼は容疑を否認しており、以前に名誉毀損訴訟でも敗訴していた。

articles.title: Virtual Romanian singer goes viral but sparks criticism
articles.description: In Romania, an AI-generated singer inspired by poems about the Roma community has become an overnight success. But critics say her creator is monetizing the woes of a marginalized group without involving its members.
articles.titleJa: 仮想のルーマニア人歌手が人気を博すが批判も受ける
articles.summary: ルーマニアでロマ族の詩に触発されたAI歌手が人気。批評家は、製作者が疎外されたグループの苦難を当事者抜きで収益化していると批判。

articles.title: Artemis II: Crew snaps 'Earthset' in throwback to Apollo 8
articles.description: The Artemis II astronauts photographed the moment when the Earth dips below the moon's horizon, as they prepare to return to our planet's gravitational pull.
articles.titleJa: アルテミス2：乗組員がアポロ8号を彷彿とさせる「地球の入り」を撮影
articles.summary: アルテミス2の宇宙飛行士たちが、地球が月の地平線に沈む瞬間を撮影。彼らは地球の重力圏に戻る準備を進めている。

articles.title: Ukraine: Several killed as Russia strikes bus, other targets
articles.description: Meanwhile, Ukrainian drone strikes on Russia and Russian-occupied regions killed at least five, including a 12-year-old boy. Ukraine has been pushing for a ceasefire during the Orthodox Easter, which is later this week.
articles.titleJa: ウクライナ：ロシアがバスなどを攻撃し、多数の死者
articles.summary: ロシアによるウクライナへの攻撃で多数が死亡。ウクライナのロシア・占領地へのドローン攻撃でも死者が出た。ウクライナは正教会の復活祭中の停戦を求めている。

articles.title: UK denies Kanye West entry, music festival canceled
articles.description: US rapper Ye, formerly called Kanye West, has been denied entry into the UK over his numerous antisemitic outbursts. He had been scheduled to headline a music festival that has now been canceled.
articles.titleJa: 英国、カニエ・ウェストの入国を拒否し音楽祭中止
articles.summary: 米ラッパーのイェ（旧カニエ・ウェスト）が、反ユダヤ主義的発言のため英国への入国を拒否され、出演予定だった音楽祭も中止となった。

articles.title: Gunman killed in shootout near Israel's Istanbul consulate
articles.description: Israel has said it will "not be intimidated" after an apparent failed attack on its consulate in Istanbul. One man was killed who the Turkish government says had links to a terror group.
articles.titleJa: イスラエル・イスタンブール領事館近くで銃撃戦、銃撃犯死亡
articles.summary: イスラエルのイスタンブール領事館近くで銃撃戦が発生し、テロ組織と関連のある男が死亡。イスラエルは威嚇されないと表明した。

articles.title: South Korea turns to EU to revive talks with the North
articles.description: Seoul's unification minister has urged Brussels to act as mediator in North-South discussions, but Pyongyang still seems to show little appetite for interaction.
articles.titleJa: 韓国、北朝鮮との対話再開に向けEUに協力を要請
articles.summary: 韓国の統一相は、北朝鮮との対話再開のためEUに仲介を要請したが、北朝鮮は依然として対話に消極的な姿勢を示している。

articles.title: US citizens beat rising energy bills with homegrown power
articles.description: In times of skyrocketing energy prices, record-breaking heat waves and blackouts, producing electricity at home has never been more appealing.
articles.titleJa: 米国民、自家発電で高騰する電気料金に対処
articles.summary: エネルギー価格の高騰、熱波、停電が続く中、米国民は電気料金を抑えるため、自宅での発電にますます注目している。

articles.title: Inside India's courts, AI's growing role sparks concern
articles.description: The increasing use of artificial intelligence in courtrooms raises worries that the technology may aggravate bias and structural distortions in the legal system.
articles.titleJa: インドの裁判所におけるAIの役割拡大に懸念
articles.summary: インドの裁判所では、AIの利用拡大が、法制度内の偏見や構造的歪みを悪化させる可能性について懸念を引き起こしている。

articles.title: One in five young Germans plan to leave the country
articles.description: A recent study shows that as many as one in five Germans between the ages of 14 and 29 are actively thinking about moving to another country — and 40% plan to do so in the long term.
articles.titleJa: ドイツの若者の5人に1人が国外移住を計画
articles.summary: ドイツの若者（14～29歳）の5人に1人が国外移住を考えており、40%が長期的な移住を計画しているとの調査結果。

articles.title: Why have US-South Africa relations soured?
articles.description: US President Donald Trump wants to sideline South Africa on the world stage. What is his issue with the country and when did things start going south?
articles.titleJa: 米国と南アフリカの関係が悪化した理由とは？
articles.summary: 米国大統領ドナルド・トランプは南アフリカを国際舞台で排除しようとしている。記事は両国関係が悪化した問題点と経緯を探る。

articles.title: ‘We still deserve due process,’ says Cambodian man deported by US to Eswatini
articles.description:
Pheap Rom was one of 15 people sent to prison in African kingdom last year despite completing US sentences
A Cambodian man deported by the US said he would have accepted being sent to Cambodia, but instead ended up imprisoned in Eswatini, a country he knew so little about that when he first read the name he thought it was another immigration detention centre in Louisiana.
Pheap Rom, who had been convicted of attempted murder, was one of 10 deportees sent to Eswatini by the US in October 2025. They joined a group of five men, from Cambodia, Cuba, Jamaica, Vietnam and Yemen, who were deported to the small southern African country in July. All were sent to a maximum-security prison. Rom was deported from Eswatini to Cambodia in March.
 Continue reading...
articles.titleJa: 米国がエスワティニに強制送還したカンボジア人男性、「適正な手続きを受ける権利がある」と訴え
articles.summary: 米国によりエスワティニに強制送還され投獄されたカンボジア人男性が、適正な手続きの必要性を訴えた。最終的にカンボジアへ送還されたが、他国への強制送還問題が浮き彫りになった。

articles.title: People of Burkina Faso should forget about democracy, says military ruler
articles.description:
Ibrahim Traoré, who took power in 2022 coup, tells state broadcaster ‘we must tell the truth, democracy isn’t for us’
People in Burkina Faso should forget about democracy as it is “not for us”, the military president, Ibrahim Traoré, told the country’s state broadcaster.
Traoré took power in a coup in September 2022, toppling another junta that had taken power just nine months earlier. He has since stifled opposition and in January banned political parties outright.
 Continue reading...
articles.titleJa: ブルキナファソの軍事支配者、「国民は民主主義を忘れるべき」と発言
articles.summary: 2022年のクーデターで政権を握ったブルキナファソの軍事大統領が、国民は民主主義を忘れるべきと発言。政治活動を抑圧し、政党を禁止している。

articles.title: Uganda receives first US deportation flight under third-country agreement
articles.description:
Dozen people arrive under new deal but legal challenges expected with scheme criticised as ‘dehumanising process’
A flight carrying people being deported from the US has landed in Uganda, as Donald Trump’s administration pushes on with its strategy of expelling migrants to countries they have no ties to.
The deported people would stay in the east African country as “a transition phase for potential onward transmission to other countries”, an unnamed senior Ugandan government official told Reuters.
 Continue reading...
articles.titleJa: 米国の強制送還便、第三国協定に基づきウガンダに初到着
articles.summary: 米国からの強制送還者を乗せた初の航空便が、第三国協定の下でウガンダに到着した。この取り決めは「非人道的なプロセス」と批判されており、法的異議申し立てが予想される。

articles.title: News outlets falsely report Somaliland called for extradition of Ilhan Omar
articles.description:
Reports, based on X post from unofficial account, follow JD Vance’s accusations and threats of finding ‘legal remedies’

Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox

Several news outlets have falsely reported that Somaliland’s government called for the extradition of Ilhan Omar, basing their stories on a post from an X account that does not represent the state despite its claims to the contrary.
Fox News, the New York Post, Sinclair Broadcast Group’s the National News Desk and the Independent ran stories on the US representative. The reports centred on a post by @RepOfSomaliland in reaction to claims by JD Vance that Omar had committed immigration fraud, which echoed prior allegations against the Somali-born Minnesota Democrat that she has vehemently denied.
 Continue reading...
articles.titleJa: 報道機関が、ソマリランドがイルハン・オマル議員の身柄引き渡しを要求したと誤報
articles.summary: 複数の報道機関が、ソマリランド政府が米下院議員イルハン・オマルの身柄引き渡しを要求したと誤報。非公式Xアカウントの投稿が元で、JDヴァンス上院議員の告発に続くもの。

articles.title: Interpol arrest warrant requested in Congo-Brazzaville for Jean-Guy Blaise Mayolas
articles.description:
Football federation president on the run with wife and son

Conviction in absentia of wide-ranging corruption charges

Authorities in Congo-Brazzaville have applied to Interpol for an international arrest warrant against Jean-Guy Blaise Mayolas, the president of the country’s football federation, Fecofoot, after he was convicted of embezzling $1.1m in Fifa funds.
Mayolas is on the run with his wife and son after they were all sentenced to life imprisonment this month for embezzling funds provided by world football’s governing body as part of its Covid-19 relief plan in February 2021. As the Guardian revealed last year, that included almost $500,000 earmarked for the Congo women’s team.
 Continue reading...
articles.titleJa: コンゴ共和国でジャン＝ギィ・ブレーズ・マヨラスに国際刑事警察機構の逮捕状が請求される
articles.summary: コンゴ共和国当局は、FIFA資金110万ドルを横領した罪で有罪判決を受けた同国サッカー連盟会長に対し、国際刑事警察機構（インターポール）の逮捕状を請求した。

articles.title: Stork warning: woman gives birth midair on Jamaica-to-New York flight
articles.description:
Baby was delivered during Caribbean Airlines flight from Kingston to the US; nationality of child to be determined

Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox

A routine passenger flight from Jamaica landed at New York’s John F Kennedy international airport with one more person than it took off with after a woman gave birth in midair, potentially setting up a tricky situation over the newborn’s citizenship.
The “medical event” occurred on a Caribbean Airlines flight from Kingston on Saturday, according to a news release from the carrier.
 Continue reading...
articles.titleJa: コウノトリ注意報：ジャマイカ発ニューヨーク行きフライトで女性が機内で出産
articles.summary: ジャマイカ発ニューヨーク行きカリビアン航空機内で女性が出産し、新生児の国籍を巡る複雑な状況が生じた。この「医療事象」により、飛行機は乗客が一人増えて到着した。

articles.title: First Nation asks court to block Alberta referendum on seceding from Canada
articles.description:
Sturgeon Lake Cree Nation has asked a court to halt the separatist push, arguing it would violate their treaty rights
A First Nation in Alberta has said that a separatist push for the province to secede from Canada is “consummately irresponsible and dishonourable” and should be shut down, arguing in court that a proposed referendum would violate their treaty rights.
A minority of residents of the oil-rich province have long argued that the province’s woes are due to the structure of payments to the federal government and a perceived inability to get their vast fossil fuel reserves to market.
 Continue reading...
articles.titleJa: ファーストネーションがアルバータ州のカナダからの分離独立住民投票を阻止するよう裁判所に要請
articles.summary: アルバータ州のスタージョンレイク・クリー・ネーションは、同州のカナダからの分離独立住民投票が条約上の権利を侵害するとして、その阻止を裁判所に求めた。

articles.title: Row over ‘virtual gated community’ AI surveillance plan in Toronto neighbourhood
articles.description:
Rosedale residents considering car licence plate-scanning Flock system in bid to tackle property crime
A row has broken out in one of Canada’s wealthiest neighbourhoods over plans to use an AI-powered surveillance system to create the country’s first “virtual gated community” to combat surging property crime.
Crime rates in Toronto as a whole are dropping but residents of Rosedale have been left on edge by a sustained rise in home invasions, with robbers targeting the tree-lined neighbourhood at a rate more than double the city average. Break-ins and thefts remain the third highest per capita in Toronto.
 Continue reading...
articles.titleJa: トロントの地域で「仮想ゲートコミュニティ」AI監視計画を巡り論争
articles.summary: トロントの裕福な地区で、AI監視システムによる「仮想ゲートコミュニティ」計画に論争が起きている。財産犯罪対策だが、プライバシー侵害への懸念がある。

articles.title: #MeToo movement brings wave of harassment claims across Colombia
articles.description:
Female journalists’ accounts of harassment trigger avalanche of allegations reaching as far as government
Juanita Gómez was reporting on an international assignment for Caracol, a Colombian television channel in 2015, when an older colleague attempted to forcibly kiss her by inside a lift.
She only managed to break free from him by pushing him away several times. Fearing any complaint would come down to the word of a “girl” against that of a senior presenter, she did not report the incident.
 Continue reading...
articles.titleJa: コロンビアで#MeToo運動がハラスメント告発の波を引き起こす
articles.summary: コロンビアで#MeToo運動が拡大し、女性ジャーナリストの告発を皮切りに、政府機関にまで及ぶハラスメントの訴えが相次いでいる。

articles.title: US is ‘using Mexico as a garbage sink’ leading to ‘toxic crisis’, UN expert says
articles.description:
Marcos Orellana, a special rapporteur, found lax environmental standards and lack of oversight allowed pollution to accumulate

Revealed: Mexico’s industrial boomtown is making goods for the US. Residents say they’re ‘breathing poison’

Mexico is facing a “toxic crisis” and has become a “garbage sink” for the US, exposing Mexican communities to dangerous pollution, a UN expert has warned.
In an interview with the Guardian and Quinto Elemento Lab, an investigative outlet, Marcos Orellana, an environmental specialist, said pollutants ranging from imported waste to dangerous pesticides were affecting people’s right to live healthy lives.
 Continue reading...
articles.titleJa: 国連専門家、米国がメキシコを「ゴミ捨て場」として利用し「毒性危機」に陥らせていると指摘
articles.summary: 国連専門家は、米国がメキシコを「ゴミ捨て場」として利用し「毒性危機」に陥らせていると警告。緩い環境基準が汚染物質の蓄積を許している。

articles.title: Construction workers block roads in France over soaring fuel prices
articles.description: Construction workers set up a roadblock on the Nantes ring road to protest rising fuel costs, saying the sharp increase is putting pressure on their businesses.
articles.titleJa: フランスで燃料価格高騰に抗議し、建設作業員が道路を封鎖
articles.summary: フランスの建設作業員が燃料価格の高騰に抗議し、ナント環状道路でバリケードを設置。燃料費の急増が事業を圧迫していると訴えた。

articles.title: Romanian football great Mircea Lucescu dies at age 80, Bucharest hospital says
articles.description: Lucescu had a lengthy coaching career and was in his second spell with the Romanian national team until stepping down last Thursday after falling ill during training.
articles.titleJa: ルーマニアのサッカー界の偉人ミルチェア・ルチェスク氏、80歳で死去とブカレストの病院が発表
articles.summary: ルーマニアのサッカー界の偉人ミルチェア・ルチェスク氏が80歳で死去した。同氏は長年にわたりコーチとして活躍し、体調不良で退任するまでルーマニア代表チームを指揮していた。

articles.title: FIFA opens disciplinary case against Spanish football federation over anti-Muslim chants
articles.description: Alongside the FIFA investigation, the Catalonia police force has opened its own probe, which is being handled by the Prosecutor's Office for Hate Crimes and Discrimination.
articles.titleJa: FIFA、反イスラムチャントを巡りスペインサッカー連盟に対する懲戒手続きを開始
articles.summary: FIFAは、反イスラムチャントを理由にスペインサッカー連盟に対する懲戒手続きを開始した。カタルーニャ警察も独自の捜査を開始し、ヘイトクライムおよび差別担当検察庁が対応している。

articles.title: Hundreds of Cuban women stage rally against US blockade as energy crisis deepens
articles.description: Cuba produces only 40% of the fuel it consumes and the shortage has paralysed the country, affecting its health system, public transportation and the production of goods and services.
articles.titleJa: キューバでエネルギー危機が深刻化する中、数百人の女性が米国による封鎖に反対する集会を開催
articles.summary: キューバでエネルギー危機が深刻化し、国内が麻痺状態にある中、数百人の女性が米国の経済封鎖に抗議する集会を開いた。燃料不足が医療、公共交通、生産に影響を与えている。

articles.title: Russia and China veto UN resolution aimed at reopening Hormuz strait as US deadline for deal looms
articles.description: One-fifth of the world's oil typically passes through the strait and Iran's chokehold during the war has sent energy prices soaring around the world.
articles.titleJa: ロシアと中国、ホルムズ海峡再開を目指す国連決議案に拒否権を行使、米国の合意期限が迫る
articles.summary: ロシアと中国が、ホルムズ海峡再開を目的とした国連決議案に拒否権を行使。海峡は世界の石油輸送の5分の1を占め、イランの支配が世界のエネルギー価格を急騰させている。

articles.title: Iran allows two French former detainees to leave the country, Macron says
articles.description: Macron has distanced France from the war in Iran, saying his country wasn't consulted in advance about the US-Israel strikes and didn’t want the war.
articles.titleJa: イラン、元フランス人被拘束者2人の出国を許可とマクロン大統領が発表
articles.summary: マクロン大統領は、イランがフランス人元被拘束者2人の出国を許可したと発表した。マクロン氏は、米国とイスラエルの攻撃について事前協議がなく、戦争を望んでいなかったと述べ、イランでの戦争からフランスを距離を置いている。

articles.title: US journalist Shelly Kittleson released by Iraqi militant group, official says
articles.description: Like many freelancers, Shelly Kittleson often worked on a shoestring budget and without the protections afforded by large news organisations to staff.
articles.titleJa: 米ジャーナリストのシェリー・キトルソン氏、イラク武装勢力に解放されると当局者が発表
articles.summary: 米国のジャーナリスト、シェリー・キトルソン氏がイラクの武装勢力に解放されたと当局者が発表した。彼女は多くのフリーランスと同様に、低予算で活動し、大手報道機関の保護を受けられなかった。

articles.title: Anti-war protesters rally outside US embassy office in Tel Aviv
articles.description: A small group of anti-war protesters gathered outside the US embassy’s Tel Aviv office, calling for an end to the conflict before police moved to disperse the demonstration.
articles.titleJa: テルアビブの米国大使館事務所前で反戦デモ隊が集会
articles.summary: テルアビブにある米国大使館事務所前で小規模な反戦デモ隊が集まり、紛争の終結を訴えた。その後、警察がデモを解散させた。

articles.title: 'Only the president knows' what he will do on Iran, White House says in statement
articles.description: Trump had threatened to target Iranian infrastructure unless Tehran agreed a deal to end the war on Tuesday, saying he was "considering blowing everything up and taking over the oil."
articles.titleJa: 「イランについて大統領だけが何をするか知っている」とホワイトハウスが声明で発表
articles.summary: ホワイトハウスは、イランに関する大統領の行動について「大統領だけが知っている」と声明を発表した。トランプ氏は、イランが戦争終結の合意に応じない限り、イランのインフラを標的にすると警告していた。

articles.title: How serious will the jet fuel crisis in Europe become?
articles.description: Jet fuel prices have increased by 95% since the United States and Israel launched military attacks against Iran on 28 February. Higher fares, fuel surcharges and reductions in capacity or limiting of unprofitable routes will be the new normal, energy analysts warn.
articles.titleJa: ヨーロッパにおけるジェット燃料危機はどれほど深刻になるのか？
articles.summary: 米国とイスラエルがイランへの軍事攻撃を開始して以来、ジェット燃料価格は95%上昇。運賃高騰、燃油サーチャージ、便数削減などが新たな常態となるとエネルギーアナリストは警告している。

articles.title: Video: Historic building in Ukraine up in flames after Russia drone attack
articles.description: A two-storey historical building in eastern Ukraine engulfed in flames after a Russian drone hit its infrastructure.
articles.titleJa: ビデオ：ウクライナの歴史的建造物がロシアのドローン攻撃で炎上
articles.summary: 東ウクライナの歴史的建造物がロシアのドローン攻撃を受け炎上。インフラに被害が出た。

articles.title: French nationals return home following Iran prison release
articles.description: Cecile Kohler and Jacques Paris were freed in November after more than three years at Evin prison.
articles.titleJa: イランの刑務所釈放後、フランス国民が帰国
articles.summary: 3年以上イランのエヴィン刑務所に収監されていたセシル・コーラーとジャック・パリスが解放され、帰国した。

articles.title: What we know about the shooting by Istanbul’s Israeli consulate
articles.description: Gunmen opened fire near the Israeli consulate in Istanbul, triggering a gun battle with Turkish police.
articles.titleJa: イスタンブールのイスラエル領事館付近での銃撃事件について分かっていること
articles.summary: イスタンブールのイスラエル領事館近くで銃撃犯が発砲し、トルコ警察と銃撃戦になった。

articles.title: Iraqi armed group releases abducted US journalist Shelly Kittleson
articles.description: The armed group Kataib Hezbollah said that it would free Kittleson on the condition that she leave Iraq immediately.
articles.titleJa: イラクの武装組織が誘拐された米国人ジャーナリスト、シェリー・キトルソンを解放
articles.summary: 武装組織カタイブ・ヒズボラが、誘拐されていた米国人ジャーナリストをイラクからの即時出国を条件に解放した。

articles.title: Tehran synagogue destroyed as US-Israeli strikes kill over a dozen
articles.description: A synagogue in Tehran was struck in a wave of US-Israeli attacks overnight.
articles.titleJa: 米国とイスラエルの攻撃で十数人が死亡、テヘランのシナゴーグが破壊される
articles.summary: テヘランのシナゴーグが、一夜にして行われた米国とイスラエルによる攻撃で破壊され、十数人が死亡した。

articles.title: Afghanistan brands China peace talks with Pakistan ‘useful’
articles.description: Kabul's foreign minister expresses hope that minor interpretations will not hinder progress.
articles.titleJa: アフガニスタン、中国とパキスタンの和平協議を「有用」と評価
articles.summary: アフガニスタンの外相は、中国とパキスタンによる和平協議を「有用」とし、進展を期待すると表明した。

articles.title: UK blocks rapper Kanye West from entry over anti-Semitism and Nazi support
articles.description: British PM Keir Starmer said it was concerning that London's Wireless Festival had booked West in the first place.

articles.title: Why JD Vance joined Pakistan’s last-ditch US-Iran mediation efforts
articles.description: Backchannel talks gain pace as Pakistan mediates and Iran seeks indirect engagement through US vice president.
articles.titleJa: JD・ヴァンスがパキスタンの土壇場での米イラン調停努力に加わった理由
articles.summary: パキスタンが米イラン間の調停を加速させ、イランが米国副大統領を通じて間接的な関与を模索している。

articles.title: WHO suspends Gaza medical evacuations after Israeli fire kills driver
articles.description: Evacuations will remain suspended until further notice, WHO says, adding that the incident is under investigation.
articles.titleJa: イスラエル軍の発砲で運転手が死亡、WHOがガザでの医療避難を停止
articles.summary: イスラエル軍の発砲により運転手が死亡したことを受け、WHOはガザでの医療避難活動を無期限に停止すると発表した。

articles.title: Top university says US-Israel attack targeted Iran’s progress, AI learning
articles.description: Iran condemns systematic attacks on civilian sites, including academic hubs, as war devastates critical infrastructure.
articles.titleJa: 米国とイスラエルの攻撃はイランの進歩とAI学習を標的にしたとトップ大学が発表
articles.summary: イランの主要大学が、米国とイスラエルによる攻撃がイランの進歩やAI学習を含む民間施設を標的にしていると非難した。

articles.title: Trump threatens to take out Iran in 'one night' if no deal before deadline
articles.description: The US president wants Tehran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global energy route, before his Tuesday deadline.

articles.title: Ten killed in Israeli strikes and clashes between Hamas and militia in Gaza, local sources say
articles.description: The Israeli strikes reportedly targeted Hamas security personnel after they clashed with an Israeli-backed militia near Maghazi camp.

articles.title: Trump issues expletive-laden threat to Iran over Hormuz Strait blockage
articles.description: The US president says he will destroy Iranian power plants and bridges if the vital waterway is not reopened.

articles.title: Trump declares victory after rescue but threats to US operation in Iran still loom
articles.description: The rescue could impact how Trump views a ground operation to take Kharg Island or to seize enriched uranium sites.

articles.title: US says it has arrested relatives of late Iranian ​general Qasem ​Soleimani
articles.description: The niece and grand-niece of Qasem Soleimani are in the custody of US Immigration and Customs Enforcement, officials said.

articles.title: French-owned ship passes through Strait of Hormuz
articles.description: It appears to be the first ship owned by a major European firm to go through the strait since the conflict began.

articles.title: US warns its citizens to leave Lebanon citing escalating security risks
articles.description: The advisory follows recent military developments, including a US jet reportedly being shot down and Israeli strikes on parts of Beirut.

articles.title: International law experts allege violations in Iran war
articles.description: The White House says it is making the entire region safer by eliminating short and long-term threats.

articles.title: Experts dispute US account of deadly Iran sports hall strike in Lamerd
articles.description: Six weapons experts have contested the US claim that video evidence suggests an Iranian missile could have hit the hall.

articles.title: 'I haven't slept for days': Iranians describe mounting desperation after a month of war
articles.description: Ordinary people from various parts of Iran describe expanding strikes, economic pain and fear of repression.

articles.title: Ringleader of suspected human trafficking network arrested in Ethiopia
articles.description: Yetbarek Dawit is alleged to have operated a multi-million-dollar operation, using torture to raise money.
articles.titleJa: エチオピアで人身売買ネットワークの首謀者が逮捕
articles.summary: エチオピアで、拷問を用いて多額の資金を調達していたとされる人身売買組織の首謀者イェットバレク・ダウィットが逮捕された。

articles.title: Dozens killed as Angola flood death toll rises
articles.description: Mourning the deaths, the president says it was a "race against the clock" to find others affected.
articles.titleJa: アンゴラで洪水による死者増加、数十人が死亡
articles.summary: アンゴラで洪水により数十人が死亡。大統領は犠牲者を悼み、被災者捜索が時間との戦いであると述べた。

articles.title: Seven Eritrean players fail to return home after international match
articles.description: Several footballers have absconded in the past leaving behind what rights groups call a repressive government.
articles.titleJa: エリトリアの選手7名が国際試合後帰国せず
articles.summary: エリトリアのサッカー選手7名が国際試合後、抑圧的な政府を理由に帰国しなかった。過去にも同様の事例がある。

articles.title: Ladysmith Black Mambazo's 'wise elder' dies aged 77
articles.description: Albert Mazibuko sang in the iconic South African choral group for more than 55 years.
articles.titleJa: レイディスミス・ブラック・マンバーゾの「賢者」77歳で死去
articles.summary: 南アフリカの著名な合唱グループ、レイディスミス・ブラック・マンバーゾで55年以上歌い続けたアルバート・マジブコ氏が77歳で死去した。

articles.title: Locals dispute Nigerian army claim of 31 rescued after Easter attack
articles.description: Five civilians were killed by the gunmen and their bodies were recovered at the scene, the army said.
articles.titleJa: イースター攻撃後、ナイジェリア軍の31人救出主張に住民が異議
articles.summary: ナイジェリアでイースター攻撃後、軍が31人を救出したと主張するが、住民がこれに異議を唱えている。銃撃犯により民間人5人が死亡した。

articles.title: Churches and politicians in South Sudan call for 'lasting peace' in Easter messages
articles.description: There are fears South Sudan may be plunged back into a civil war less than a decade after it ended.
articles.titleJa: 南スーダンで教会と政治家がイースターメッセージで「永続的な平和」を訴え
articles.summary: 南スーダンでは、内戦終結から10年も経たずに再び内戦に陥る懸念が高まる中、教会や政治家がイースターのメッセージで「永続的な平和」を呼びかけた。

articles.title: 'I adore her now': Mother learns to live with child's autism in a country with little help
articles.description: Malawian Martha Ongwane, brought low by her daughter's autism, found a rare support group.
articles.titleJa: 「今は彼女をとても愛している」：支援が少ない国で自閉症の子供と共に生きることを学ぶ母親
articles.summary: マラウイのマーサ・オングワネさんは、娘の自閉症に苦しんだ後、希少な支援グループを見つけ、共に生きることを学んだ。

articles.title: 'We want a voice in our land' - the people evicted to build Nigeria's capital
articles.description: People evicted to build Nigeria's capital accuse the government of failing to fulfill its promises to them.
articles.titleJa: 「私たちの土地で声を上げたい」：ナイジェリアの首都建設のために立ち退かされた人々
articles.summary: ナイジェリアの首都建設のために立ち退きを強いられた人々が、政府が約束を履行していないと非難し、彼らの土地での発言権を求めている。

articles.title: One ant for $220: The new frontier of wildlife trafficking
articles.description: The craze for collecting ants takes Kenya by surprise as smugglers zone in to make a profit.
articles.titleJa: アリ一匹220ドル：野生生物密売の新たなフロンティア
articles.summary: アリ収集のブームがケニアで野生生物密売の新たな分野となり、密輸業者らが利益を得ようと狙っている。

articles.title: 'The gravest crime against humanity': What does the UN vote on slavery mean?
articles.description: African and Caribbean nations want countries which benefited from slavery to pay compensation but it will not be straightforward.
articles.titleJa: 「人道に対する最も重大な犯罪」：国連の奴隷制に関する投票は何を意味するのか？
articles.summary: アフリカ諸国とカリブ諸国は、奴隷制から恩恵を受けた国々に対し賠償を求めているが、国連での採決は一筋縄ではいかないだろう。

articles.title: DRC: Urban trees are natural CO₂ stores that merit closer attention
articles.description: Urban trees are not just landscaping features. They are natural carbon sinks, capable of offsetting human activity-related carbon dioxide (CO₂) emissions.
articles.titleJa: DRC: 都市の木々はより注目に値する天然のCO₂貯蔵庫
articles.summary: コンゴ民主共和国では、都市の木々が単なる景観ではなく、人間の活動によるCO₂排出量を相殺する天然の炭素吸収源として、その重要性が高まっている。

articles.title: How initiatives to narrow Africa’s digital gap are affecting women’s representation
articles.description: Africa Wiki Women encourages African women’s advancement in online knowledge creation and sharing through training, contribution campaigns, and mentorship programs.

articles.title: How AI is used to influence global elections
articles.description: Companies that work in political persuasion are experimenting with AI at scale to see how they can make the traditional influencing methods even cheaper, faster, more convincing, and more automated.
articles.titleJa: AIは世界の選挙にどう影響を与えるか
articles.summary: 政治的説得に携わる企業は、AIを大規模に活用し、従来の選挙影響手法をより安価、迅速、説得力のあるものに自動化しようと世界的に試みている。

articles.title: Unpacking the political complexities of the Benue killings in Nigeria
articles.description: What we are dealing with here in Benue is a calculated, well planned, full-scale genocidal invasion and land grabbing campaign by Herder terrorists and bandits, which ... is worsening every year.
articles.titleJa: ナイジェリアのベヌエ州における殺戮事件の政治的複雑性を解き明かす
articles.summary: ナイジェリアのベヌエ州で発生している殺戮は、牧畜民テロリストによる計画的な民族浄化と土地強奪であり、毎年悪化している深刻な政治的対立である。

articles.title: Cameroonian rice farmers caught between national ambition and rural realities
articles.description: The Cameroonian government’s rice self-sufficiency policy has sparked enthusiasm among the country’s rice farmers. In the northwest, rice farmers combine courage with determination every day to fulfil these national promises.
articles.titleJa: カメルーンの米農家、国家の野心と地方の現実の間で
articles.summary: カメルーン政府の米自給自足政策は農家の熱意を呼んだが、北西部の農家は国家の約束を果たすべく、日々現実と奮闘しており、課題に直面している。

articles.title: African women make economic gains through global gig economy
articles.description: For decades, economic opportunity was often tied to migration. Today, millions of Africans are plugging directly into global commerce from their homes and building careers that span continents.
articles.titleJa: アフリカの女性がグローバルギグエコノミーを通じて経済的利益を得る
articles.summary: アフリカの女性たちは、グローバルなギグエコノミーを通じて自宅から直接国際的な商業に参加し、経済的な機会と大陸をまたぐキャリアを築いている。

articles.title: Climate change brings changes to North Kivu’s agricultural practices in the DRC
articles.description: Climate change has impacted North Kivu, a region in the eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC), where farmers have had to adapt their farming practices to survive.
articles.titleJa: 気候変動がDRC北キブ州の農業慣行に変化をもたらす
articles.summary: コンゴ民主共和国東部の北キブ州では気候変動が深刻な影響を与え、農家は生き残るために農業慣行の適応を余儀なくされている。

articles.title: François Kaserake Kamate on global complicity and the fight for the DRC
articles.description: "I was of violence, war and the cycle of social injustices. I wanted to follow a non-violent path because I believed in peaceful strategies to change the paradigm."
articles.titleJa: フランソワ・カセラケ・カマテ、世界の共謀とDRCのための闘いについて語る
articles.summary: フランソワ・カセラケ・カマテは、暴力と社会的不公正の経験から、パラダイムを変えるために非暴力と平和的戦略を信じ、コンゴ民主共和国のために闘っている。

articles.title: From menstrual dignity to digital safety: How grassroots feminists are redefining gender justice
articles.description: What connects a pad box in a Nigerian school, a coalition meeting in Pakistan, and a digital security workshop in Paraguay? They each address a structural barrier that limits women’s participation.
articles.titleJa: 月経の尊厳からデジタル安全まで：草の根フェミニストがいかにジェンダー平等を再定義しているか
articles.summary: 草の根フェミニストたちは、月経の尊厳、デジタル安全保障など多様な課題を通じて、女性の参加を妨げる構造的障壁に対処し、ジェンダー平等を世界的に再定義している。

articles.title: Ethiopia’s urban renewal projects and turn toward aesthetic propaganda
articles.description: This is not a trend limited to Ethiopia. Influencers have increasingly become important in popularizing selective authoritarian stories from Dubai to China, and thereby legitimizing authoritarian narratives.
articles.titleJa: エチオピアの都市再生プロジェクトと美的プロパガンダへの転換
articles.summary: エチオピアの都市再生プロジェクトは、権威主義的な物語を正当化するための「美的プロパガンダ」として利用されており、これはドバイから中国まで見られる世界的な傾向である。

articles.title: Who is Viktor Orbán, Hungarian PM fighting to stay in power after 16 years?
articles.description: Since 2010, Orbán has transformed Hungary into what the European Parliament has denounced as a "hybrid regime of electoral autocracy".
articles.titleJa: 16年間政権を握るハンガリー首相ヴィクトル・オルバンとは？
articles.summary: ハンガリーのヴィクトル・オルバン首相は16年間政権にあり、欧州議会は同国を「選挙制独裁のハイブリッド体制」と非難している。

articles.title: More than 1,700 Brits who fell ill in Cape Verde join action against Tui
articles.description: Tui is investigating the claims and says it is "not in a position to provide a statement at this stage".
articles.titleJa: カーボベルデで体調を崩した1,700人以上の英国人がTuiに対して訴訟を起こす
articles.summary: カーボベルデで病気になった1,700人以上の英国人観光客が旅行会社Tuiに対し訴訟を提起。Tuiは現在調査中と発表。

articles.title: Spain's huge pork industry seeks salvation from swine fever threat
articles.description: Countries around the world, including the US, have already stopped imports over the outbreak.
articles.titleJa: スペインの巨大な豚肉産業、豚熱の脅威から救済を求める
articles.summary: スペインの豚肉産業は豚熱の脅威に直面し、世界各国が輸入を停止。業界は対策を講じ、救済を求めている。

articles.title: Pogacar may face fine for running red light in Flanders win
articles.description: Tadej Pogacar wins a record-equalling third Tour of Flanders, but could face action after he is one of up to 20 riders who run a red light at a railway crossing.
articles.titleJa: ポガチャル、フランドル勝利で赤信号無視により罰金の可能性
articles.summary: タデイ・ポガチャル選手はツール・デ・フランドルで優勝したが、赤信号を無視したため罰金を科される可能性がある。

articles.title: Pope Leo calls for global leaders to choose peace in his first Easter Mass
articles.description: Pope Leo XIV addressed thousands of worshippers gathered in St Peter's Square on Easter Sunday in his first address as pontiff.
articles.titleJa: レオ法王、初のイースターミサで世界の指導者に平和の選択を呼びかける
articles.summary: レオ14世法王は初のイースターミサで、サンピエトロ広場に集まった数千人の信者に対し、世界の指導者が平和を選択するよう訴えた。

articles.title: Russian attack on Ukraine market kills five
articles.description: A Russian drone hit a busy spot in the southern Ukrainian town on Saturday morning, injuring another 21 people.
articles.titleJa: ロシアのウクライナ市場攻撃で5人死亡
articles.summary: ロシアのドローンがウクライナ南部の市場を攻撃し、5人が死亡、21人が負傷した。

articles.title: Hungary alleges plot to blow up gas pipeline ahead of election
articles.description: It comes a week before votes are cast and follows warnings of so-called false flag operations blamed on Ukraine.
articles.titleJa: ハンガリー、選挙を前にガスパイプライン爆破計画を主張
articles.summary: 選挙を1週間後に控え、ハンガリーがガスパイプラインの爆破計画を主張。ウクライナに責任を負わせる偽旗作戦の警告も出ていた。

articles.title: AI videos fuel rhetoric as Orbán bids for four more years in Hungary
articles.description: Videos have targeted Viktor Orbán's election rival, who could unseat him after 16 years in office.
articles.titleJa: AI動画がオルバン氏のハンガリー再選運動のレトリックを加速させる
articles.summary: ハンガリーでは、ヴィクトル・オルバン氏の再選運動においてAI動画が対立候補を標的にし、政権奪取の可能性を巡るレトリックを加熱させている。

articles.title: German males under 45 may need military approval for long stays abroad
articles.description: Under the law, travel approvals must generally be granted and it remains unclear how the rule would be enforced if breached.
articles.titleJa: ドイツ、45歳未満の男性は長期海外滞在に軍の承認が必要になる可能性
articles.summary: ドイツでは、45歳未満の男性が長期で海外に滞在する際、軍の承認が必要になる可能性のある法律が検討されている。

articles.title: Faced with new energy shock, Europe asks if reviving nuclear is the answer
articles.description: As war drives up gas and fuel prices, Europeans turn again to the issue of energy independence.
articles.titleJa: 新たなエネルギーショックに直面し、ヨーロッパは原子力発電の復活が解決策かと問う
articles.summary: 戦争によるガス・燃料価格の高騰を受け、ヨーロッパは新たなエネルギー危機に直面。エネルギー自立のため、原子力発電の復活が議論されている。

articles.title: Minnesota deny Rodriguez has rare muscle wasting condition
articles.description: Minnesota United deny rumours that midfielder James Rodriguez is suffering from a potentially life-threatening muscle-wasting condition.

articles.title: Football rally in Peru leaves one dead and dozens injured
articles.description: Hundreds of Alianza Lima fans had gathered for a pregame rally ahead of a match against local rivals Universitario de Deportes.

articles.title: Cuba begins releasing more than 2,000 prisoners as US pressure mounts
articles.description: US President Trump has made clear his desire to change Cuba's leadership and has placed the country under an oil embargo.

articles.title: US lifts sanctions on Venezuelan interim leader Delcy Rodríguez
articles.description: It is the latest sign of warming relations between the US and Venezuela after Nicolás Maduro was seized in a commando raid.

articles.title: New Brazil law allows separated couples joint custody over pets
articles.description: Lawmakers in the Brazilian Congress view the law change as a reflection of pets' importance for their owners.

articles.title: Brazil judge blocks Sugarloaf Mountain zipline
articles.description: The project to build a zipline linking the popular Rio tourist spot to Morro da Urca proved controversial.

articles.title: In one of country's poorest states, Venezuelans hope for post-Maduro boom
articles.description: Ione Wells travels to Sucre, where a lack of investment has hampered locals' prospects.

articles.title: Cuba's mothers-to-be prepare to give birth in a country plunged into darkness
articles.description: Two pregnant women tell the BBC's Will Grant of their hopes and fears as their nation is mired in crisis.

articles.title: Colombia's budding tech scene needs a cash boost
articles.description: Colombia has become a tech hub for Latin America, but attracting investors is a challenge.

articles.title: Young Venezuelans voice hope and frustration as post-Maduro future unfolds
articles.description: Young people who have only known one political system are adjusting to change after US forces seized Maduro.

articles.title: Community micro-hydropower plants revolutionise isolated areas of the Dominican Republic
articles.description: Community micro-hydropower projects in the DR rely on local leadership to deliver energy from renewable sources to isolated areas excluded from national distribution systems, contributing to reduced greenhouse gas emissions.

articles.title: How Bad Bunny brought the issue of Puerto Rico’s power grid into world view
articles.description: Puerto Rico’s electricity system has faced repeated crises since 2017’s Hurricane Maria, which destroyed much of the grid and triggered the longest blackout in modern U.S. history; outages remain routine.

articles.title: Ecuador’s Netanyahu and Trump supporters target Ecuadorian–Iranian Cultural Center
articles.description: Wars no longer have defined frontlines. Battlefields can be anywhere those in power decide that a neighbor, due to their faith, ethnicity, or background, is an enemy.

articles.title: Concerns grow as Jamaica ends Cuban medical programme
articles.description: Terminating the decades-old programme that brought hundreds of Cuban medical personnel to its shores is an unusual move, especially for a programme that had been running smoothly for 50 years.

articles.title: How far-right ‘fear tactics’ affect girls seeking legal abortion in Brazil
articles.description: Fighting a resolution to ensure access to legal abortion for girls under 14 is the latest example of abortion rights being used as a political tool by conservatives in the country

articles.title: Nawat language now has its own Wikipedia
articles.description: The New Year brought an exciting announcement: the official launch of Wikipedia in Nawat, the result of several years of training and community-building across communities in El Salvador and beyond.

articles.title: Beyond words: Incarcerated women’s responses to punitive systems in Peru
articles.description: A collage-based artistic creation workshop revealed how a group of incarcerated women counter the dynamics and effects of imprisonment through individual reflection, creative practice, and the sustaining of collective bonds.

articles.title: Action images from Venezuela serve as political messaging and narrative control for Trump
articles.description: For professor Clotilde Perez, spoken to by Pública, contemporary geopolitics has become “media-performative”, where no image is neutral.

articles.title: Mexican government partially unblocks secure internet
articles.description: The Tor network is used by millions of users daily to securely access the internet, safeguard their right to information and freedom of expression, and avoid censorship or government surveillance.

articles.title: Healing a region: How healthcare is bringing peace to Arauca, Colombia
articles.description: Reliable, inclusive healthcare does more than treat illness. It can function as social glue that softens community strain and transforms clinics into safe spaces for everyone.

articles.title: Trump's deadline looms but Asian nations already have deals with Iran
articles.description: Nations in the region have been keen to reach agreements as their economies are heavily reliant on Middle East energy.

articles.title: Air India CEO steps down early as losses mount
articles.description: Wilson, whose term was set to end in 2027, will remain CEO and MD until a successor is appointed.

articles.title: India's high-growth economy gets a Middle East oil shock
articles.description: India's currency, stocks and growth projections take a beating as the country faces a triple energy shock due to Iran war.

articles.title: India's top court hears challenges to ruling on women's entry into temple
articles.description: Experts say the court's decision will have far-reaching consequences for women's religious freedoms in India.

articles.title: Nine policemen sentenced to death in India over Covid custody killings
articles.description: The father and son died in jail after being arrested on suspicion of breaching Covid lockdown rules in 2020.

articles.title: Emergency jabs after 100 children die of suspected measles in a month in Bangladesh
articles.description: More than 100 people, mostly children, have been killed by measles since mid-March, officials suspect.

articles.title: Iconic 19th Century painting sets Indian art record with $17.9m sale
articles.description: Yashoda and Krishna, painted by Raja Ravi Varma, beat a record set by an MF Husain work last year.

articles.title: Magnitude 7.4 earthquake hits off Indonesia, killing one
articles.description: The quake struck between the islands of Manado and Ternate. Tsunami warnings have now been lifted.

articles.title: How China fell for a lobster: What an AI assistant tells us about Beijing's ambition
articles.description: The AI agent sparked a frenzy of "raising lobsters" in March, with users training the tool to suit their needs.

articles.title: Fears cost of water and beer to soar as India's scorching summer hits
articles.description: Makers of plastic and glass bottles are struggling to access raw materials as the war squeezes supplies.

articles.title: Members participating in IFD Agreement issue joint ministerial declaration at end of MC14
articles.description: The 129 WTO members participating in the Investment Facilitation for Development Agreement (IFDA) issued a joint ministerial declaration on 29 March at the end of the 14th Ministerial Conference in Yaoundé, Cameroon. A joint press release by the IFDA co-Coordinators — the Republic of Korea and Chile — is provided below.

articles.title: MC14 concludes with adopted decisions, progress on key outstanding issues
articles.description: The WTO's 14th Ministerial Conference (MC14) concluded early 30 March with ministers adopting a number of decisions as well as making a commitment to continue work in Geneva on key outstanding issues.

articles.title: DG cites MPIA as "practical, confidence-building bridge" pending dispute reform deal
articles.description: The Multi-Party Interim Appeal Arbitration Arrangement (MPIA) is serving as a practical, confidence building bridge for WTO members committed to a fully functioning dispute settlement system pending an agreement on dispute settlement reform, Director-General Ngozi Okonjo-Iweala said on 28 March. She made her remarks at a meeting of parties to the MPIA on the sidelines of the WTO's 14th Ministerial Conference (MC14).

articles.title: Ministers exchange views on key WTO topics, consider paths forward at MC14
articles.description: Ministers and senior officials attending the WTO's 14th Ministerial Conference (MC14) participated in a series of meetings on 28 March to address key issues on the WTO agenda and to provide political direction for the post-MC14 work ahead.

articles.title: Members adopt a pathway to bring E‑Commerce Agreement into force via interim arrangements
articles.description: Sixty-six members, covering approximately 70% of global trade, have adopted a pathway to bring into force the WTO Agreement on Electronic Commerce through interim arrangements while continuing to work towards its incorporation into the WTO legal framework of rules, co-convenors announced on 28 March at the 14th Ministerial Conference taking place in Yaoundé, Cameroon. The joint press release is below.

articles.title: Adding value to cotton in Africa can drive inclusive growth, ministers and agency heads say
articles.description: Investment in the cotton, textiles and garments sector can help transform economic development in Central and West Africa, said speakers at a high-level event on the eve of the WTO's 14th Ministerial Conference in Yaoundé, Cameroon, on 25 March and at the opening ceremony on 26 March. Ministers, heads of international agencies, development finance institutions, private sector partners and FIFA representatives highlighted the opportunities for Africa to weave its way up the value chain to accelerate economic growth.

articles.title: Australia contributes an additional AUD 2 million to WTO Fish Fund
articles.description: The government of Australia has committed an additional AUD 2 million (approximately CHF 1.2 million) to the WTO Fisheries Funding Mechanism, known as the Fish Fund, to assist developing and least-developed members in implementing the Agreement on Fisheries Subsidies. This contribution builds on Australia's earlier contribution of AUD 2 million in May 2023.

articles.title: EU launches safeguard investigation on grain-oriented electrical steel
articles.description: On 27 March 2026, the European Union notified the WTO’s Committee on Safeguards that it had initiated, on the same day (27 March 2026), a safeguard investigation on imports of grain-oriented electrical steel.

articles.title: Africa, Latin America strengthen economic and trade relations at MC14 side event
articles.description: A high-level meeting dedicated to reinforcing cooperation and trade relations between Africa and Latin America was held on the margins of the WTO's 14th Ministerial Conference (MC14) on 27 March. In a keynote address, Director-General Ngozi Okonjo-Iweala highlighted the importance of this initiative to diversify trade and continue improving South-South trade.

articles.title: Plastics Dialogue coordinators issue MC14 ministerial statement, technical outcome package
articles.description: On 26 March, the opening day of the 14th Ministerial Conference (MC14), the six co-coordinators of the Dialogue on Plastics Pollution and Environmentally Sustainable Plastics Trade (DPP) — Australia, Barbados, China, Ecuador, Fiji and Morocco — presented a ministerial statement and a package of five technical documents. Together, they reflect the progress achieved by participating members since MC13 and set out a forward-looking roadmap for trade-related cooperation to address plastics pollution.
